beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee and toast at home.
beats.reasoning: Yesterday's low energy (40/100) and late gaming session means a slower morning; Sebastian wakes late enough to avoid the pre-dawn commute rush, but early enough to prep for work.

beats.activity: Drive to Redmond clinic, taking I-90 eastbound to avoid the SR-520 bridge closure.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon; Sebastian leaves early enough to avoid peak closure chaos, but takes the longer I-90 route instead of his usual bridge crossing.

beats.activity: Morning and early afternoon patient sessions at the sports clinic.
beats.reasoning: Standard clinic day; the drizzle and rain keep him indoors and focused; no lunch break slip-out today (unlike yesterday's World Cup detour).

beats.activity: Grab lunch at Redmond Town Center, eating at a covered food court to avoid the rain.
beats.reasoning: Rain is steady until 2pm; Sebastian stays sheltered indoors, grabs something quick, and uses the time to decompress after morning sessions.

beats.activity: Afternoon patient sessions and admin work at the clinic.
beats.reasoning: Full afternoon block; rain clears by 2pm but Sebastian stays focused on work through end of shift.

beats.activity: Drive back to Seattle on I-90, traffic lighter now that SR-520 closure has ended.
beats.reasoning: Post-work commute; SR-520 closure ended at noon, so return trip is straightforward; Sebastian takes I-90 again to stay consistent.

beats.activity: Pop into Scarecrow Video (Capitol Hill) for a quick browse on the way home.
beats.reasoning: Yesterday's thrifting high still lingers; Sebastian's curiosity draws him to Capitol Hill for a short detour—he's hunting for obscure DVDs and loves the hunt.
Short drive from Capitol Hill back to home; Sebastian settles in for the evening.

beats.activity: Cook dinner (simple pasta or stir-fry) and eat at home.
beats.reasoning: Low energy from yesterday carries over; Sebastian keeps dinner routine simple and grounding.

beats.activity: Wind down with video games, checking out the DVD haul from Scarecrow.
beats.reasoning: Matches yesterday's vibe: post-dinner gaming and media consumption; a low-key evening after a full work day.
7+ hours of sleep to recover from yesterday's late night and a full work day; beat spans past midnight as required.

diary: Woke up to that grey drizzle and knew today was going to be one of those slow-burn Tuesday mornings. The SR-520 closure had everyone talking at the clinic—I'd already rerouted to I-90, which turned out fine. Sessions back-to-back kept me moving: a runner with tendonitis, a weekend warrior's shoulder. The rain drummed on the clinic windows the whole time. Grabbed lunch under cover at Redmond Town Center, just wanted something warm and quiet. Afternoon flew by with paperwork and one last patient before heading home. Traffic was actually decent by then. Detoured through Scarecrow on Capitol Hill—needed that little hit of normalcy, browsing the shelves for a bit. Cooked some pasta at home, nothing fancy. Spent the evening gaming, just letting the day settle. There's something about rainy Tuesdays that makes everything feel smaller and more manageable.
thought_process: The bridge closure threw a wrench into the usual routine, but I'd anticipated it—checked traffic before leaving. Staying proactive with commute planning let me focus on work without stress. The clinic was steady, maybe a bit busier because of the weather keeping people indoors or the closure backing up schedules. I needed that lunch break to decompress. The Scarecrow stop was impulsive but deliberate—I wasn't ready to go straight home, needed a transition moment. Gaming tonight felt earned. The grey weather actually settled my mood; no pressure to do anything ambitious, just exist and recover. Tomorrow will probably be clearer.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
